understanding.goal_understanding: The goal was to solve each level within a limited step budget by moving the selectable piece(s) around a 3-pixels-per-cell grid to reach goal tiles/positions (marked as + in the semantic grid), potentially using multiple selectables and pushing blocks as needed to clear paths or satisfy level conditions.
understanding.mechanics_understanding: Each MOVE action attempted to shift the currently selected piece by one cell (3 pixels) in the chosen direction, sometimes failing if blocked by walls/boundaries or other objects. Pushable objects (e.g., blocks) could be pushed if they were immediately adjacent in the move direction and the space beyond them allowed the push; otherwise the move would have no effect. Walls acted as immovable blockers, but some boundaries could behave inconsistently and needed to be tested rather than trusted purely via the movement_blocked hints. When multiple selectables existed, CLICK with an object's exact pixel-grid position switched control to that selectable; subsequent MOVE actions applied to the newly selected piece.
